ontology.foodon_gap_note: No FoodOn entry exists specifically for roti prata or chocolate prata. Mapped to the nearest parent class 'flatbread food product' as a defensible approximate anchor.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 code A0FKP (Flat bread) is the closest defensible category. No specific code exists for roti prata or chocolate-topped flatbread variants in the FoodEx2 hierarchy.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No SNOMED CT code identified for roti prata or chocolate prata. SNOMED CT does not maintain granular codes for Southeast Asian street-food flatbreads.

allergens: milk (if ghee or margarine contains dairy)

serving.description: 1 piece of prata with chocolate topping
serving.natural_language_portion: One standard serving of chocolate prata is approximately 200 g, equivalent to one full piece of fried flatbread with chocolate drizzle, as commonly served at Singapore hawker stalls and mamak restaurants.

nutrient_source.title: Roti Prata (Plain) — Health Promotion Board Singapore Foodo Nutrient Composition Database; supplemented by USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy: Bread, chapati or roti, plain (FDC ID 175036)
nutrient_source.organization: Health Promotion Board, Singapore; United States Department of Agriculture
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Core macros (energy, protein, fat, carbohydrate, sugar, sodium, dietary fibre, potassium, calcium, magnesium, iron, cholesterol) are sourced from HPB Singapore Foodo for plain roti prata (confidence 0.95). Saturated fat (3.5 g/100 g) is adapted from USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy entry for plain chapati/roti (FDC ID 175036), which is the closest approved-source equivalent for the fat profile of a pan-fried wheat flatbread made with ghee or margarine. Trans fat is set to -1 as USDA SR Legacy does not report trans fat for this entry and no approved source provides a verified value for this specific preparation. Vitamin C is set to 0.0 as USDA SR Legacy reports negligible vitamin C for plain wheat flatbread. The chocolate topping adds marginal sugar and fat not fully captured in the HPB plain prata entry; values are treated as approximate for the chocolate variant.

gi_source.title: Atkinson FS, Foster-Powell K, Brand-Miller JC. International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2008. Diabetes Care. 2008;31(12):2281-2283. Chapati / roti (wheat flour flatbread) GI reference values.
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for chocolate prata specifically. The University of Sydney GI Database and the peer-reviewed International Tables of Glycemic Index (Atkinson et al., Diabetes Care, 2008) report GI values of approximately 52–70 for plain Indian wheat flatbreads (chapati, roti, paratha) tested under standard conditions. A GI of 62 is used as a central defensible estimate for plain roti prata, consistent with the medium-GI range for pan-fried wheat flatbreads. The chocolate drizzle adds a small sugar load but does not materially alter the GI of the bread base. Glycemic load per serving calculated as: GL = (GI × carbohydrate_g per serving) / 100 = (62 × 150.46) / 100 = 93.3, for a 200 g serving (75.23 g carbohydrate per 100 g × 2 = 150.46 g carbohydrate per serving).

hypertension.key_factor: Sodium 632 mg per 100 g exceeds the 600 mg/100 g upper threshold; a 200 g serving delivers approximately 1264 mg sodium, representing over half the recommended daily sodium limit of 2000 mg.
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should avoid or strictly limit chocolate prata; if consumed, request reduced salt in dough preparation and limit to half a piece, and avoid adding curry gravy which further increases sodium load.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Total carbohydrate is high at 75.2 g per 100 g; however, dietary fibre is 10.2 g per 100 g which moderates glycaemic response, and the estimated GI of 62 places this in the medium-GI category.
type2_diabetes.guidance: Individuals with type 2 diabetes should limit portion to one small piece (approximately 100 g), choose plain prata over chocolate variants to reduce added sugar, monitor post-meal blood glucose, and pair with a protein source to blunt the glycaemic response.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Saturated fat at 3.5 g per 100 g and potential trans fat from margarine used in dough preparation both raise LDL cholesterol; sodium at 632 mg per 100 g further elevates cardiovascular risk.
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Individuals with cardiovascular disease should minimise consumption of chocolate prata; if eaten, request preparation with ghee or vegetable oil instead of margarine to reduce trans fat exposure, limit to half a piece, and avoid high-sodium accompaniments such as curry.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: Potential trans fat from margarine used in dough preparation is pro-inflammatory and directly relevant to RA disease activity; sodium at 632 mg per 100 g also exceeds the RA-relevant threshold.
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: Individuals with rheumatoid arthritis should avoid chocolate prata prepared with margarine; request ghee or vegetable oil substitution, limit portion size, and reduce chocolate sauce to minimise refined sugar intake which promotes inflammatory mediators.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Chocolate prata is a Singaporean hawker and mamak restaurant dish consisting of a flaky, multi-layered pan-fried flatbread made from wheat flour dough enriched with ghee or margarine, topped with chocolate sauce after cooking. It is a sweet variant of the classic roti prata (also known as roti canai in Malaysia), which is a staple of Indian-Muslim and Malay cuisine in Singapore. The dough is stretched and folded repeatedly to create thin, laminated layers, then cooked on a flat iron griddle until golden and crisp on the outside while remaining soft and chewy inside. The chocolate drizzle adds sweetness and a mild cocoa flavour. Nutritionally, chocolate prata is energy-dense at approximately 412 kcal per 100 g, with a high carbohydrate content of 75.2 g per 100 g and moderate protein at 9.85 g per 100 g. Dietary fibre is notably high at 10.2 g per 100 g, which moderates the glycaemic response despite the high total carbohydrate. Sodium is elevated at 632 mg per 100 g, making it a concern for individuals managing hypertension. Saturated fat at 3.5 g per 100 g is clinically relevant for cardiovascular disease management, and margarine-based preparations may introduce trans fat. A standard serving is approximately 200 g (one piece), delivering a glycaemic load of approximately 93. Chocolate prata is commonly eaten at breakfast, lunch, or as a late-night supper snack in Singapore, often accompanied by curry gravy, sugar, or condensed milk.
common_user_queries: How many calories in chocolate prata?
What is the difference between prata and paratha?

confidence_reason: Core macros (energy, protein, fat, carbohydrate, sugar, sodium, dietary fibre, potassium, calcium, magnesium, iron, cholesterol) are sourced from HPB Singapore Foodo for plain roti prata at confidence 0.95. Saturated fat is adapted from USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy for plain chapati/roti (FDC ID 175036). Trans fat is set to -1 as no approved source provides a verified value. Vitamin C is set to 0.0 per USDA SR Legacy for plain wheat flatbread. The chocolate variant introduces additional sugar and fat from the topping not fully captured in the HPB plain prata entry. GI is an analogous estimate from the University of Sydney GI Database and peer-reviewed literature for wheat flatbreads. Overall confidence is medium due to the adapted source match and the absence of a chocolate-prata-specific approved source entry.
notes: monounsaturated_fat_g and polyunsaturated_fat_g set to -1: detailed fatty acid breakdown for roti prata is not available in the HPB Singapore Foodo database or USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy as of 2026. trans_fat_g set to -1: USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy does not report trans fat for plain chapati/roti (FDC ID 175036), and no other approved source provides a verified trans fat value for this specific preparation; margarine-based preparation may introduce trans fat but a confirmed numeric value from an approved source is unavailable. water_g set to -1: moisture content for this preparation is not reported in the HPB Foodo entry. vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg set to -1: these micronutrients are not reported in the HPB Foodo plain roti prata entry and no approved secondary source provides reliable values for this specific preparation. cholesterol_mg is 0.0 as reported by HPB Foodo for plain roti prata; this is a confirmed value from the approved source. vitamin_c_mg is 0.0 per USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy for plain wheat flatbread (FDC ID 175036); confirmed negligible. saturated_fat_g of 3.5 g per 100 g is adapted from USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy for plain chapati/roti as the closest approved-source equivalent; the input record cited grabfood at 5.0 g per 100 g which is not an approved source and has been replaced. The cultural classification in the input record lists primary_cuisine as Malay; however, roti prata is more accurately classified as Singaporean Indian (mamak) cuisine with strong Malay cultural adoption. Both ethnic contexts are acknowledged.

improvement_actions_taken: Iteration 2: Corrected ontology match types from 'approximate' to 'closest_parent'; added 'Saakalet Paraattaa' to flat aliases array for consistency with aliases_by_language.romanized; updated nutrient_source notes to acknowledge Grabfood provenance.

Iteration 3: Replaced forbidden Grabfood-sourced nutrient values with approved-source equivalents. saturated_fat_g replaced from 5.0 (Grabfood) to 3.5 (USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy, FDC ID 175036, plain chapati/roti). trans_fat_g replaced from 0.25 (Grabfood) to -1 (no approved source reports this value). vitamin_c_mg replaced from 2.5 (Grabfood) to 0.0 (USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy, negligible for plain wheat flatbread). nutrient_source updated to reference only HPB Singapore Foodo and USDA FoodData Central as approved sources, with no mention of Grabfood as a source. gi_source updated to explicitly cite Atkinson et al. (2008) Diabetes Care peer-reviewed publication alongside University of Sydney GI Database to ensure GI source compliance. notes updated to document all -1 fields including trans_fat_g with approved-source justification.
